P99: Latency value where 99% of requests were faster than this number.
TTFT: Time to first token, measured when the first useful model output is observed.
SSE: Server-Sent Events, an HTTP streaming format used by many LLM APIs.
zero-copy: Engineering style that avoids duplicating bytes in memory unless required.
lock-free: Concurrency technique that avoids traditional locks, usually using atomics.
micro-batching: Writing many telemetry records together instead of one write per request.
critical path: The minimum work required to respond to the client.
backpressure: Refusing or slowing new work before the system collapses.
